title: Narendra Modi sworn in as Prime Minister for third consecutive term
text: New Delhi (PTI) - Narendra Modi was sworn in as Prime Minister of India for the third consecutive time on June 9, 2024, at Rashtrapati Bhavan. President Droupadi Murmu administered the oath of office to Modi, who led the National Democratic Alliance to victory in the 2024 general elections. The NDA secured 293 seats in the 543-member Lok Sabha. Several Cabinet ministers were also sworn in during the ceremony. The swearing-in was attended by leaders from neighbouring countries including Bangladesh, Sri Lanka, Nepal, and the Maldives as part of India's neighbourhood-first foreign policy.
subject: Politics

title: Parliament passes Women's Reservation Bill guaranteeing 33 percent seats
text: New Delhi (PTI) - The Indian Parliament passed the Constitution (One Hundred and Twenty-Eighth Amendment) Bill, 2023, commonly known as the Women's Reservation Bill, which reserves 33 percent of seats in the Lok Sabha and state legislative assemblies for women. The Rajya Sabha passed the bill with 214 votes in favour and no opposition. Prime Minister Narendra Modi called it a historic moment for Indian democracy. The law will come into effect after the next delimitation exercise.
subject: Politics

title: Congress wins Karnataka assembly elections securing 135 seats
text: Bengaluru (PTI) - The Indian National Congress won a decisive majority in the Karnataka assembly elections, securing 135 seats in the 224-member assembly, according to the Election Commission of India. The Bharatiya Janata Party was reduced to 66 seats while the Janata Dal (Secular) won 19 seats. Congress leader Siddaramaiah was later sworn in as Chief Minister. The results marked a significant setback for the BJP which had governed the state.
subject: Politics

title: Arvind Kejriwal arrested by Enforcement Directorate in Delhi liquor policy case
text: New Delhi (PTI) - Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal was arrested by the Enforcement Directorate on Thursday in connection with the alleged Delhi excise policy scam. This made Kejriwal the first sitting Chief Minister to be arrested in India. The ED alleged that proceeds of crime were used to fund election campaigns. The Aam Aadmi Party called the arrest politically motivated. The Delhi High Court later examined his custody conditions.
subject: Politics

title: BJP wins Uttar Pradesh assembly elections with massive majority
text: Lucknow (PTI) - The Bharatiya Janata Party won a massive majority in the Uttar Pradesh assembly elections, securing 255 seats out of 403. The Samajwadi Party won 111 seats. Yogi Adityanath was sworn in as Chief Minister for the second term. The election result was seen as a significant endorsement of the BJP's governance in the state and a strong indicator ahead of the 2024 general elections.
subject: Politics

title: India elects Droupadi Murmu as 15th President, first tribal woman to hold office
text: New Delhi (PTI) - Droupadi Murmu was elected as the 15th President of India, becoming the first tribal woman and the second woman to hold the country's highest constitutional office. She defeated opposition candidate Yashwant Sinha by securing 64 percent of the total valid votes. Born in a tribal family in Odisha's Mayurbhanj district, Murmu was the former Governor of Jharkhand. She was sworn in on July 25, 2022.
subject: Politics

title: Rahul Gandhi disqualified from Lok Sabha after conviction in defamation case
text: New Delhi (PTI) - Congress leader Rahul Gandhi was disqualified from the Lok Sabha after a Surat court convicted him in a 2019 criminal defamation case over his Modi surname remarks. The disqualification came into effect immediately after the conviction. Gandhi later appealed against the conviction in the Supreme Court, which stayed the conviction and restored his membership. The episode triggered a political controversy across the country.
subject: Politics

title: Election Commission announces 2024 Lok Sabha election schedule across seven phases
text: New Delhi (PTI) - The Election Commission of India announced the schedule for the 18th General Elections to be held in seven phases between April 19 and June 1, 2024. The counting of votes was scheduled for June 4, 2024. The announcement set in motion the Model Code of Conduct across the country. Over 960 million voters were eligible to participate in the elections, making it the world's largest democratic exercise.
subject: Politics

title: AAP sweeps Delhi assembly elections winning 62 of 70 seats
text: New Delhi (PTI) - The Aam Aadmi Party led by Arvind Kejriwal swept the Delhi assembly elections, winning 62 of the 70 seats. The BJP won eight seats while the Congress drew a blank for the second consecutive time. Kejriwal was sworn in as Chief Minister for the third time. The result was seen as a strong mandate for AAP's governance model focused on free electricity, water, and improvements in government schools and hospitals.
subject: Politics

title: TMC wins West Bengal assembly elections, Mamata Banerjee retains power
text: Kolkata (PTI) - The Trinamool Congress won the West Bengal assembly elections for the third consecutive time, securing 213 seats in the 294-seat assembly. The BJP, which had mounted an aggressive campaign, won 77 seats. Mamata Banerjee was sworn in as Chief Minister for the third term. The election was marked by violence in several districts and high voter turnout. The Supreme Court took note of post-poll violence in the state.
subject: Politics

title: Modi government launches PM Kisan Samman Nidhi scheme for farmers
text: New Delhi (PTI) - Prime Minister Narendra Modi launched the Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi scheme, under which eligible farmer families would receive Rs 6,000 per year in direct income support, paid in three equal instalments. The scheme was announced in the interim budget and was made applicable to all farmer families across the country. The government said the scheme would benefit approximately 120 million farming families across India.
subject: Politics

title: India and China agree to disengage troops from all friction points in eastern Ladakh
text: New Delhi (PTI) - India and China reached an agreement on patrolling arrangements along the Line of Actual Control in eastern Ladakh, effectively ending the over four-year military standoff that began in May 2020. The Indian Army confirmed that disengagement had been completed at the Depsang and Demchok friction points. The agreement was announced by the Ministry of External Affairs. The two countries agreed to resume normal patrolling activities.
subject: Politics

title: Amit Shah introduces Citizenship Amendment Act in Parliament
text: New Delhi (PTI) - Home Minister Amit Shah introduced the Citizenship Amendment Bill in the Lok Sabha, which proposed to grant Indian citizenship to persecuted minorities from Pakistan, Bangladesh and Afghanistan. The bill was passed by the Lok Sabha with 311 votes in favour and 80 against. The bill excluded Muslims from its ambit, triggering widespread protests across the country particularly in the Northeast and universities.
subject: Politics

title: Supreme Court upholds abrogation of Article 370 in Jammu and Kashmir
text: New Delhi (PTI) - The Supreme Court of India unanimously upheld the constitutional validity of the abrogation of Article 370, which had granted special status to Jammu and Kashmir. A five-judge constitution bench led by Chief Justice D Y Chandrachud also directed the Election Commission to hold assembly elections in J&K by September 30, 2024. The court said the President had the power to abrogate Article 370.
subject: Politics

title: India's Opposition forms INDIA alliance ahead of 2024 general elections
text: Mumbai (PTI) - Twenty-six opposition parties came together to form the Indian National Developmental Inclusive Alliance, abbreviated as INDIA, to contest the 2024 general elections jointly against the ruling BJP-led NDA. The alliance was announced after a meeting in Mumbai. The member parties included the Indian National Congress, Trinamool Congress, DMK, Aam Aadmi Party, Samajwadi Party, NCP, Shiv Sena and others.
subject: Politics

title: Pulwama terror attack kills 40 CRPF jawans in Jammu and Kashmir
text: Pulwama (PTI) - A suicide bomber rammed an explosive-laden vehicle into a CRPF convoy on the Jammu-Srinagar National Highway at Pulwama in Jammu and Kashmir, killing at least 40 paramilitary personnel and injuring several others. Pakistan-based terrorist organisation Jaish-e-Mohammed claimed responsibility for the attack. The attack triggered massive outrage across the country and led to severe strain in India-Pakistan relations. The government vowed strong action against the perpetrators.
subject: Security

title: Indian Air Force conducts airstrikes on Balakot terror camp in Pakistan
text: New Delhi (PTI) - The Indian Air Force conducted airstrikes on a Jaish-e-Mohammed terrorist training camp in Balakot in Pakistan's Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province, in retaliation for the Pulwama terror attack. The strikes were carried out in the early hours and were described as a non-military preemptive action. Foreign Secretary Vijay Gokhale briefed ambassadors in New Delhi about the strikes. Pakistan denied any damage to the camp.
subject: Security

title: Uri army base attack kills 18 soldiers, India launches surgical strikes
text: Uri (PTI) - Militants attacked an Indian Army brigade headquarters at Uri in Jammu and Kashmir's Baramulla district, killing 18 soldiers and injuring 19 others in one of the deadliest attacks on the armed forces in recent years. The militants stormed the base and set fire to tents before being neutralised. Following the attack, Indian Army special forces conducted surgical strikes across the Line of Control against launch pads used by militants.
subject: Security

title: NIA arrests key conspirator in 2006 Mumbai train blasts case
text: Mumbai (PTI) - The National Investigation Agency arrested a key conspirator in the July 11, 2006 Mumbai suburban railway serial bomb blasts that killed 188 people and injured more than 800 others. The seven coordinated explosions occurred within 11 minutes across the Western Railway line during the evening rush hour. Several accused including members of the Student Islamic Movement of India and Lashkar-e-Taiba were convicted by a special court.
subject: Security

title: Pathankot airbase attack foiled, five terrorists killed by security forces
text: Pathankot (PTI) - Security forces killed five terrorists who had attacked the Pathankot Air Force Station in Punjab in a sustained operation lasting several days. The terrorists had entered India from Pakistan and targeted the strategic airbase. Two security personnel were also killed in the operation. The National Investigation Agency probed the attack and the government summoned the Pakistani High Commissioner to lodge a strong protest.
subject: Security

title: Maoist attack kills 22 CRPF personnel in Chhattisgarh's Sukma district
text: Raipur (PTI) - A group of Maoist insurgents ambushed and killed 22 CRPF personnel in Sukma district of Chhattisgarh in one of the deadliest attacks on security forces in the region. The personnel were on a road opening patrol when they were ambushed by the CPI Maoist guerrillas. The attack triggered a massive combing operation in the region. Senior officials said the security forces had been lured into the ambush.
subject: Security

title: Terrorist attack on Pahalgam kills 26 tourists in Jammu and Kashmir
text: Pahalgam (PTI) - Terrorists opened fire on tourists at Baisaran meadow near Pahalgam in Anantnag district of Jammu and Kashmir, killing 26 people including tourists from various states and a local guide. The attack was one of the deadliest on civilians in the valley in two decades. Security forces launched a massive manhunt for the attackers. The incident triggered nationwide outrage and a diplomatic crisis with Pakistan.
subject: Security

title: Coimbatore car bomb blast kills one and injures several near temple
text: Coimbatore (PTI) - A car exploded near the Kottai Eachanari Vinayagar Temple in Coimbatore killing the lone occupant of the vehicle and injuring several bystanders. The National Investigation Agency took over the probe and arrested multiple persons with alleged links to the Popular Front of India. The case was linked to an alleged larger conspiracy. The police said the incident was a case of a vehicle being used as an improvised explosive device.
subject: Security

title: Security forces kill top Hizbul Mujahideen commander in Kashmir encounter
text: Srinagar (PTI) - Security forces killed a top commander of the Hizbul Mujahideen militant organisation in an encounter in the Sopore area of north Kashmir. The militant commander was among those responsible for several attacks on security forces and civilians in the valley. The Army and J&K Police jointly conducted the operation. The encounter lasted several hours before the security forces neutralised the militants.
subject: Security

title: India bans Popular Front of India under UAPA for alleged terror links
text: New Delhi (PTI) - The Indian government banned the Popular Front of India and its associated organisations for five years under the Unlawful Activities Prevention Act, citing alleged links with terrorist organisations and activities aimed at destabilising the country. The ban followed a nationwide crackdown in which hundreds of PFI members were arrested across multiple states. Home Minister Amit Shah said the organisation was involved in radicalisation of youth.
subject: Security

title: Manipur violence displaces thousands as ethnic clashes continue for months
text: Imphal (PTI) - Ethnic violence between the Meitei and Kuki-Zo communities in Manipur continued to displace thousands of people across the state as the conflict entered its second month. More than 200 people were killed and over 50,000 displaced in one of the worst episodes of internal violence in northeast India in decades. The army and central paramilitary forces were deployed in large numbers. The Supreme Court took suo motu cognisance of the situation.
subject: Security

title: India becomes world's fifth largest economy surpassing UK
text: New Delhi (PTI) - India overtook the United Kingdom to become the world's fifth largest economy in terms of GDP at current market prices, according to data from the International Monetary Fund. India's GDP stood at approximately 3.53 trillion dollars against the UK's 3.38 trillion dollars. The milestone was widely reported and celebrated as a sign of India's growing economic clout. India is now behind only the United States, China, Germany, and Japan.
subject: Economy

title: RBI raises repo rate by 50 basis points to tame inflation
text: Mumbai (PTI) - The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee raised the policy repo rate by 50 basis points to 5.9 percent in its ongoing effort to tame retail inflation which had remained above the 6 percent tolerance band for several months. RBI Governor Shaktikanta Das said the MPC voted unanimously to remain focused on the withdrawal of accommodation to ensure that inflation progressively aligns with the target. GDP growth forecast was revised.
subject: Economy

title: Government launches Rs 20 lakh crore economic package to fight COVID-19 impact
text: New Delhi (PTI) - Prime Minister Narendra Modi announced a special economic package of Rs 20 lakh crore, equivalent to about 10 percent of India's GDP, to help the country revive from the economic damage caused by the COVID-19 pandemic and the lockdown. The package was announced by Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman in five tranches over several days. It included measures for MSMEs, migrant workers, farmers, and the financial sector.
subject: Economy

title: India's GDP contracts 23.9 percent in first quarter due to COVID lockdown
text: New Delhi (PTI) - India's gross domestic product contracted by 23.9 percent in the April-June quarter of 2020-21, the worst quarterly decline since the country began publishing quarterly GDP data, according to the National Statistical Office. The contraction was attributed to the nationwide lockdown imposed to contain the spread of COVID-19. The manufacturing sector contracted by 39.3 percent while the construction sector contracted by 50.3 percent.
subject: Economy

title: Adani Group stocks lose Rs 10 lakh crore market value after Hindenburg report
text: Mumbai (PTI) - Shares of Adani Group companies lost a combined market capitalisation of over Rs 10 lakh crore in a matter of days following the release of a report by US short-seller Hindenburg Research alleging stock manipulation and accounting fraud. The Adani Group denied all allegations and called the report a malicious combination of selective misinformation. The Supreme Court later constituted an expert committee to examine the matter.
subject: Economy

title: Government introduces new labour codes consolidating 44 central laws
text: New Delhi (PTI) - The Indian Parliament passed four new labour codes that consolidate 44 existing central labour laws into a simplified framework. The codes cover wages, industrial relations, social security, and occupational safety. Trade unions raised concerns about certain provisions including changes to conditions for layoffs and strikes in larger establishments. The government said the reforms would improve ease of doing business and protect worker rights.
subject: Economy

title: Sensex hits all-time high of 80,000 points for the first time in history
text: Mumbai (PTI) - The BSE Sensex crossed the historic milestone of 80,000 points for the first time, reflecting strong investor confidence in the Indian economy. The rally was led by gains in banking, information technology, and fast-moving consumer goods stocks. Analysts attributed the rise to strong corporate earnings, robust foreign institutional investor inflows, and expectations of political stability following the general elections. The Nifty 50 also hit a record high.
subject: Economy

title: India's forex reserves touch record high of 704 billion dollars
text: Mumbai (PTI) - India's foreign exchange reserves touched a record high of 704.88 billion dollars, according to data released by the Reserve Bank of India. The increase was attributed to a rise in foreign currency assets as well as gold reserves. Analysts said the comfortable level of reserves provided adequate import cover and acted as a buffer against external shocks. India ranks among the top five countries globally in terms of foreign exchange reserves.
subject: Economy

title: GST collections cross Rs 2 lakh crore mark for the first time
text: New Delhi (PTI) - Goods and Services Tax collections crossed the Rs 2 lakh crore mark for the first time in April 2024, according to the Finance Ministry. The collections stood at Rs 2.10 lakh crore, reflecting strong economic activity and improved compliance. Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman attributed the milestone to increased consumption and better tax administration. GST was introduced in July 2017 replacing multiple state and central indirect taxes.
subject: Economy

title: Nirmala Sitharaman presents sixth consecutive Union Budget
text: New Delhi (PTI) - Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman presented the Union Budget for 2024-25, her sixth consecutive budget and a record for any finance minister in India. The budget increased capital expenditure to Rs 11.11 lakh crore and announced significant tax relief for the middle class under the new tax regime. She announced increased allocations for infrastructure, agriculture, and education. The fiscal deficit was pegged at 5.1 percent of GDP.
subject: Economy

title: India reports over four lakh COVID cases in a single day at second wave peak
text: New Delhi (PTI) - India reported a record 4,14,188 new COVID-19 cases in a single day as the second wave of the pandemic swept across the country, overwhelming the healthcare system. Hospitals in major cities including Delhi and Mumbai faced acute shortages of oxygen, ICU beds, and critical medicines. The death toll also reached a record high with 3,915 deaths reported in a single day. Multiple states imposed lockdowns and curfews to control the spread.
subject: Health

title: India launches world's largest COVID-19 vaccination drive
text: New Delhi (PTI) - India launched the world's largest COVID-19 vaccination drive, beginning with the inoculation of healthcare workers across the country. Prime Minister Narendra Modi addressed the nation and declared the drive a success in the making. Two vaccines — Covishield developed by the Serum Institute of India and Covaxin developed by Bharat Biotech — were approved for emergency use. India aimed to vaccinate 300 million people by July 2021.
subject: Health

title: India imposes nationwide lockdown to contain COVID-19 spread
text: New Delhi (PTI) - Prime Minister Narendra Modi announced a complete nationwide lockdown for 21 days to contain the spread of COVID-19, giving citizens only four hours notice before the restrictions came into effect. The lockdown required all citizens to stay home except for essential services. It was one of the most stringent lockdowns in the world. The announcement triggered a mass exodus of migrant workers from cities to their home states.
subject: Health

title: India achieves milestone of 2 billion COVID-19 vaccine doses administered
text: New Delhi (PTI) - India achieved the milestone of administering 2 billion COVID-19 vaccine doses since the launch of its vaccination programme, making it the first country after China to cross this mark. The Union Health Ministry said this achievement was a testament to India's robust vaccination delivery infrastructure. Prime Minister Modi congratulated health workers and scientists. India had vaccinated the majority of its eligible adult population.
subject: Health

title: Omicron variant drives third wave of COVID across India
text: New Delhi (PTI) - India's third wave of COVID-19 driven by the Omicron variant saw daily case counts rise sharply across the country with major cities including Mumbai, Delhi, and Kolkata reporting the highest numbers. However, hospitalisation rates remained significantly lower than in the second wave. The government reimposed several restrictions including night curfews in multiple states. Health officials urged eligible citizens to receive booster doses.
subject: Health

title: Chandrayaan-3 successfully lands near moon's south pole, India makes history
text: Bengaluru (ISRO) - India's Chandrayaan-3 spacecraft successfully landed near the south pole of the Moon, making India the fourth country in the world and the first to land near the lunar south pole. The Vikram lander touched down and subsequently deployed the Pragyan rover, which began exploring the lunar surface. Prime Minister Modi, who was attending the BRICS summit in South Africa, joined the celebration virtually. ISRO scientists erupted in joy at the Mission Operations Complex.
subject: Science

title: ISRO successfully launches Aditya-L1 solar mission
text: Sriharikota (ISRO) - The Indian Space Research Organisation successfully launched the Aditya-L1 spacecraft, India's first space-based solar observatory, aboard the PSLV-C57 rocket from the Satish Dhawan Space Centre in Sriharikota. The spacecraft is destined for the Sun-Earth Lagrangian point 1, located approximately 1.5 million kilometres from Earth, from where it will study the Sun's outer atmosphere, solar winds, and solar flares.
subject: Science

title: Chandrayaan-2 orbiter continues to function successfully around moon
text: Bengaluru (ISRO) - India's Chandrayaan-2 orbiter continues to function successfully in lunar orbit nearly five years after it was launched, according to ISRO. While the Vikram lander crashed during its soft landing attempt in September 2019, the orbiter has been sending valuable data from its eight scientific instruments. ISRO said the orbiter has a mission life of several more years and has contributed significant data on the lunar surface and exosphere.
subject: Science

title: India successfully tests Agni-5 missile with multiple independently targetable warheads
text: New Delhi (PTI) - India successfully conducted a flight test of the Agni-5 ballistic missile equipped with Multiple Independently Targetable Re-entry Vehicle technology, becoming one of the few countries to possess this capability. MIRV technology allows a single missile to carry multiple warheads that can hit different targets. The test was conducted from APJ Abdul Kalam Island off the Odisha coast. Defence Minister Rajnath Singh congratulated DRDO and the armed forces.
subject: Science

title: IIT researchers develop low-cost COVID testing kit with 96 percent accuracy
text: New Delhi (PTI) - Researchers at the Indian Institute of Technology, Delhi developed a low-cost rapid diagnostic kit for detecting COVID-19 with an accuracy of 96 percent. The kit, which could deliver results within one hour at a fraction of the cost of standard RT-PCR tests, was submitted for regulatory approval to the Indian Council of Medical Research. The development was funded by the Department of Science and Technology and offered hope for mass testing in rural areas.
subject: Science

title: ISRO's PSLV-C55 successfully places TeLEOS-2 and Lumelite-4 in orbit
text: Sriharikota (ISRO) - India's Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle PSLV-C55 successfully placed Singapore's TeLEOS-2 and Lumelite-4 satellites into their designated orbits from the Satish Dhawan Space Centre. The mission demonstrated India's growing capabilities as a commercial satellite launch provider. ISRO Chairman S Somanath said the successful launch further established India's position as a reliable partner for international space missions.
subject: Science

title: India launches NavIC navigation satellite to enhance regional positioning system
text: Sriharikota (ISRO) - India successfully launched the NVS-01 navigation satellite, the first of the second generation of the Navigation with Indian Constellation system, using the GSLV-F12 rocket. The NVS-01 carries an L1 signal payload for the first time, making NavIC signals compatible with civilian receivers worldwide. NavIC provides positioning, navigation, and timing services over India and the surrounding region.
subject: Science

title: Cyclone Amphan devastates West Bengal and Odisha killing over 80
text: Kolkata (PTI) - Super Cyclone Amphan, the most powerful storm to form in the Bay of Bengal in two decades, made landfall between Digha and Hatibania near the Sunderban delta causing widespread devastation across West Bengal and Odisha. Over 80 people were killed and millions were rendered homeless. The storm caused damage estimated at over Rs 1 lakh crore. The Army and NDRF were deployed for rescue and relief operations.
subject: Disaster

title: Uttarakhand floods claim over 200 lives in worst monsoon disaster in years
text: Dehradun (PTI) - Flash floods and landslides triggered by extremely heavy monsoon rainfall killed more than 200 people across Uttarakhand in one of the worst natural disasters to hit the hill state in years. Several villages were swept away and hundreds of people remained missing. The Army, NDRF, and SDRF launched massive rescue operations. The disaster caused widespread damage to roads, bridges, and infrastructure in the mountain state.
subject: Disaster

title: Cyclone Tauktae hits Gujarat coast, kills over 150 across western India
text: Ahmedabad (PTI) - Extremely severe cyclonic storm Tauktae made landfall near Una in Gujarat's Gir Somnath district with wind speeds of up to 175 kilometres per hour, killing over 150 people across Goa, Maharashtra, Karnataka, and Gujarat. A naval vessel with hundreds of COVID patients aboard sank off the Mumbai coast during the storm. Thousands were evacuated ahead of the landfall in the largest pre-cyclone evacuation in years.
subject: Disaster

title: Chamoli glacier burst triggers massive floods in Uttarakhand, kills over 70
text: Dehradun (PTI) - A massive flood swept through the Rishiganga and Dhauliganga river valleys in Uttarakhand's Chamoli district after a glacier burst triggered a wall of water and debris. The flood destroyed a dam under construction on the Rishiganga river and severely damaged the Tapovan-Vishnugad hydroelectric project. More than 70 people were killed and over 100 remained missing. The disaster raised questions about construction in ecologically fragile mountain zones.
subject: Disaster

title: Cyclone Biparjoy makes landfall in Kutch, thousands evacuated
text: Ahmedabad (PTI) - Extremely severe cyclonic storm Biparjoy made landfall near Jakhau port in Kutch district of Gujarat after more than 100,000 people were evacuated from coastal areas of Gujarat and Rajasthan. The storm caused significant damage to infrastructure and disrupted power supply in several districts. No major loss of life was reported due to the pre-emptive evacuations. The NDRF deployed 34 teams for relief and rescue operations.
subject: Disaster

title: India wins T20 World Cup 2024, defeating South Africa in final
text: Bridgetown (PTI) - India won the ICC Men's T20 World Cup 2024 for the second time by defeating South Africa by seven runs in the final at Kensington Oval in Barbados. Virat Kohli scored a crucial 76 runs and was named Player of the Match. India remained unbeaten throughout the tournament. The victory came after an eleven-year wait for an ICC title. Captain Rohit Sharma and coach Rahul Dravid were given a heroes' welcome on their return to India.
subject: Sports

title: Neeraj Chopra wins gold medal in javelin throw at Tokyo Olympics
text: Tokyo (PTI) - Neeraj Chopra became only the second Indian athlete to win an individual gold medal at the Olympic Games when he threw the javelin a distance of 87.58 metres at the Tokyo Summer Olympics. The 23-year-old from Haryana produced his best throw in the first attempt and held on to clinch India's first ever gold medal in athletics at the Olympics. The victory was celebrated across India. He was subsequently awarded the Major Dhyan Chand Khel Ratna award.
subject: Sports

title: India beat Pakistan in World Cup 2023 match by seven wickets
text: Ahmedabad (PTI) - India defeated Pakistan by seven wickets in the ICC Cricket World Cup 2023 group stage match at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad. Jasprit Bumrah and Kuldeep Yadav wreaked havoc with the ball as Pakistan were bowled out for 191. India chased the target with more than 10 overs to spare. Rohit Sharma scored 86 runs. The match was watched by a record crowd of over 100,000 at the stadium.
subject: Sports

title: PV Sindhu becomes first Indian to win two Olympic medals winning silver
text: Tokyo (PTI) - PV Sindhu became the first Indian to win two Olympic medals when she won a bronze medal at the Tokyo Summer Olympics, adding to the silver she had won at the Rio Olympics in 2016. Sindhu defeated China's He Bingjiao in the bronze medal match of the women's singles badminton event. She had been defeated in the semifinal by the eventual gold medallist. The achievement cemented her status as India's greatest shuttler.
subject: Sports

title: India hosts G20 Summit successfully under its presidency
text: New Delhi (PTI) - India successfully hosted the G20 Leaders' Summit at Bharat Mandapam in New Delhi under its presidency. The summit adopted the New Delhi Declaration by consensus, with all 20 member countries endorsing the document. Prime Minister Modi announced the addition of the African Union as a permanent member of the G20. The summit was attended by leaders including US President Joe Biden, UK Prime Minister Rishi Sunak, and French President Emmanuel Macron.
subject: Politics

title: Virat Kohli announces retirement from Test cricket
text: New Delhi (PTI) - Star Indian cricketer Virat Kohli announced his retirement from Test cricket in a post on social media. Kohli, who is widely regarded as one of the greatest batsmen in the history of the game, scored 9,230 runs in 123 Test matches including 30 centuries. He captained the Indian team to several memorable Test victories including a historic series win in Australia. His retirement marked the end of an era in Indian cricket.
subject: Sports

title: Mirabai Chanu wins silver medal in weightlifting at Tokyo Olympics
text: Tokyo (PTI) - Saikhom Mirabai Chanu won a silver medal in the 49 kg category in weightlifting at the Tokyo Summer Olympics, giving India its first medal of the Games. Chanu lifted a total weight of 202 kg in the clean and jerk and snatch disciplines. The 26-year-old from Manipur had won gold at the 2017 World Weightlifting Championships. Her medal was India's first in Olympic weightlifting since Karnam Malleswari won bronze at the Sydney Olympics in 2000.
subject: Sports

title: Supreme Court upholds right to privacy as fundamental right
text: New Delhi (PTI) - A nine-judge constitution bench of the Supreme Court of India unanimously held that the right to privacy is a fundamental right protected under the Indian Constitution. The landmark judgment was delivered in the context of challenges to the Aadhaar biometric identity scheme. Chief Justice J S Khehar and eight other judges wrote separate concurring opinions. The ruling has far-reaching implications for data protection law in India.
subject: Judiciary

title: Supreme Court decriminalises Section 377, legalises same-sex relationships
text: New Delhi (PTI) - A five-judge constitution bench of the Supreme Court unanimously struck down the part of Section 377 of the Indian Penal Code that criminalised consensual homosexual acts between adults. Chief Justice Dipak Misra, writing the lead judgment, said the colonial-era law was a violation of fundamental rights including the right to equality, dignity, and privacy. The verdict was hailed as historic by LGBTQ activists across the country.
subject: Judiciary

title: Supreme Court verdict paves way for Ram temple construction in Ayodhya
text: New Delhi (PTI) - The Supreme Court in a unanimous verdict by a five-judge constitution bench directed that the disputed 2.77-acre site at Ayodhya be handed over to a trust to build a Ram temple. The court also directed the government to allot an alternative five-acre plot to the Sunni Waqf Board for a mosque. The 1,045-page judgment declared that the demolition of the Babri Masjid in 1992 was illegal and a violation of law.
subject: Judiciary

title: Ram Mandir consecration ceremony held in Ayodhya, PM Modi performs pran pratishtha
text: Ayodhya (PTI) - Prime Minister Narendra Modi performed the pran pratishtha or consecration ceremony of Ram Lalla at the newly constructed Ram temple in Ayodhya in the presence of thousands of saints, dignitaries, and prominent citizens from across India. The ceremony marked the culmination of a decades-long movement for the construction of a temple at the birthplace of Lord Ram. The event was televised nationally and celebrated across the country.
subject: Social

title: Nirbhaya case convicts hanged after seven years of legal battle
text: New Delhi (PTI) - The four convicts in the December 2012 Delhi gang rape case, commonly known as the Nirbhaya case, were hanged at Tihar Jail at 5:30 am after a prolonged legal battle spanning more than seven years. The convicts — Mukesh Singh, Pawan Gupta, Vinay Sharma, and Akshay Kumar Singh — were convicted of gang-raping a 23-year-old paramedic student on a bus and throwing her off. The hanging came after all legal remedies were exhausted.
subject: Judiciary

title: India passes Personal Data Protection Bill in Parliament
text: New Delhi (PTI) - The Indian Parliament passed the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, providing a legal framework for the collection, processing, and storage of digital personal data of Indian citizens. The law establishes a Data Protection Board and introduces the concept of data principals and data fiduciaries. It was widely seen as India's equivalent of the European Union's General Data Protection Regulation. Civil society groups raised concerns about exemptions granted to the government.
subject: Judiciary

title: Farmers protest ends after government agrees to repeal three farm laws
text: New Delhi (PTI) - Prime Minister Narendra Modi announced the repeal of the three controversial farm laws that had triggered a year-long protest by farmers mainly from Punjab, Haryana, and western Uttar Pradesh at the borders of Delhi. Modi said the government had failed to convince a section of farmers about the benefits of the laws and hence decided to repeal them. Parliament subsequently passed the Farm Laws Repeal Bill. The farmers' unions called off their protests.
subject: Social

title: India passes Waqf Amendment Bill amid protests from Muslim groups
text: New Delhi (PTI) - The Indian Parliament passed the Waqf (Amendment) Bill despite protests from Muslim groups and opposition parties who said the bill interfered with the administration of Islamic charitable endowments. The government said the bill was aimed at bringing transparency and accountability to the Waqf Boards. Critics argued that the bill gave excessive powers to government-appointed members in Waqf Boards. The bill was referred to a joint parliamentary committee before being passed.
subject: Social

title: Reliance Industries becomes first Indian company to cross Rs 20 lakh crore market cap
text: Mumbai (PTI) - Reliance Industries Limited became the first Indian company to cross the market capitalisation of Rs 20 lakh crore on the stock exchanges, cementing its position as India's most valuable company by a wide margin. The conglomerate led by Mukesh Ambani has interests spanning petrochemicals, refining, retail, telecom, and digital services. Reliance's Jio Platforms had already attracted massive investments from global technology and private equity funds.
subject: Business

title: Tata Motors completes acquisition of Jaguar Land Rover from Ford
text: Mumbai (PTI) - Tata Motors completed the acquisition of Jaguar Land Rover from Ford Motor Company for 2.3 billion dollars in one of the largest overseas acquisitions by an Indian company at the time. The deal gave Tata Motors ownership of two of Britain's most prestigious automobile brands. The acquisition was initially seen as risky but JLR later became a major profit driver for Tata Motors, contributing significantly to the group's global revenues.
subject: Business

title: UPI transactions cross Rs 20 lakh crore in December 2023
text: New Delhi (PTI) - Unified Payments Interface transactions crossed the milestone of Rs 20 lakh crore in value during December 2023, according to data released by the National Payments Corporation of India. A total of 12.02 billion UPI transactions were recorded during the month, with a value of Rs 18.23 lakh crore. The Finance Ministry said the achievement reflected the success of India's digital payment infrastructure which has become a model for the world.
subject: Business

title: Byju's faces insolvency proceedings as creditors move NCLT
text: New Delhi (PTI) - The National Company Law Tribunal admitted an insolvency petition against education technology company Byju's filed by the Board of Control for Cricket in India over non-payment of dues. The development marked a dramatic fall from grace for what was once India's most valuable startup. Byju's had faced a series of controversies including delayed financial results, allegations of financial mismanagement, and disputes with lenders over loan covenants.
subject: Business

title: India bans 59 Chinese apps including TikTok citing security concerns
text: New Delhi (PTI) - The Indian government banned 59 mobile applications developed by Chinese companies, including the popular short video platform TikTok, citing concerns about national security and the privacy of Indian users. The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology said the apps were engaged in activities that were prejudicial to India's sovereignty, integrity, defence, and public order. The ban came amid heightened tensions along the China-India border in Ladakh.
subject: Business

title: India pledges net zero emissions by 2070 at COP26 climate summit
text: Glasgow (PTI) - Prime Minister Narendra Modi announced at the COP26 climate summit in Glasgow that India would achieve net zero carbon emissions by 2070. He also announced that India would meet 50 percent of its energy requirements from renewable sources by 2030, increase non-fossil fuel energy capacity to 500 gigawatts by 2030, and reduce the total projected carbon emissions by one billion tonnes by the same year. The pledges were seen as significant from a major developing economy.
subject: Environment

title: Delhi records worst air quality in three years as AQI crosses 450
text: New Delhi (PTI) - Delhi's air quality deteriorated to its worst level in three years with the Air Quality Index crossing 450, placing it firmly in the severe plus or emergency category. Crop burning in neighbouring Punjab and Haryana combined with unfavourable meteorological conditions and vehicular and industrial emissions were blamed for the toxic smog. Schools were shut and construction activities banned. The Supreme Court summoned officials to address the annual crisis.
subject: Environment

title: India's renewable energy capacity crosses 200 gigawatt milestone
text: New Delhi (PTI) - India's total installed renewable energy capacity crossed the 200 gigawatt milestone, according to the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy, making it the fourth largest renewable energy country in the world. Solar energy accounted for the largest share of the renewable capacity, followed by wind, hydro, and bioenergy. India has set a target of achieving 500 gigawatts of non-fossil fuel-based energy capacity by 2030.
subject: Environment

title: Serum Institute of India emerges as world's largest vaccine manufacturer
text: Pune (PTI) - The Serum Institute of India based in Pune emerged as the world's largest vaccine manufacturer by volume, producing more than 1.5 billion doses of various vaccines annually. The institute, led by Adar Poonawalla, played a critical role in the global response to the COVID-19 pandemic by producing the Covishield vaccine under license from AstraZeneca and Oxford University. India supplied vaccines to over 100 countries through the COVAX programme.
subject: Health

title: India eradicates wild poliovirus, WHO certifies country polio-free
text: New Delhi (WHO) - The World Health Organisation certified India as free of wild poliovirus, a major public health milestone for a country that once accounted for the majority of global polio cases. The last case of wild poliovirus in India was recorded in January 2011. The achievement was attributed to the success of the Pulse Polio immunisation campaign which vaccinated hundreds of millions of children. India was declared polio-free by the WHO in 2014.
subject: Health

title: AIIMS doctors perform rare heart transplant surgery on infant
text: New Delhi (PTI) - Doctors at the All India Institute of Medical Sciences in New Delhi successfully performed a rare heart transplant surgery on a six-month-old infant, one of the youngest recipients of a heart transplant in the country. The surgery lasted over eight hours and involved a team of 20 doctors and nurses. The infant, who suffered from a congenital heart defect, recovered well after the procedure. The team was led by a senior cardiac surgeon.
subject: Health

title: India inaugurates first Vande Bharat Express semi-high-speed train
text: New Delhi (PTI) - Prime Minister Narendra Modi flagged off the first Vande Bharat Express, India's first semi-high-speed self-propelled passenger train, from the New Delhi railway station. The train, manufactured at the Integral Coach Factory in Chennai, can achieve speeds of up to 160 kilometres per hour. It operates on the New Delhi to Varanasi route. The Train 18, as it was originally called, was developed under the Make in India initiative at a fraction of the cost of imported trains.
subject: Infrastructure

title: Mumbai-Ahmedabad bullet train project work begins
text: Ahmedabad (PTI) - Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Japanese Prime Minister Shinzo Abe jointly laid the foundation stone for the Mumbai-Ahmedabad high-speed rail corridor, India's first bullet train project. The project is being built with Japanese technical expertise and a soft loan from the Japan International Cooperation Agency. The 508-kilometre corridor will allow travel at speeds of up to 320 kilometres per hour, reducing the journey time from eight hours to around three hours.
subject: Infrastructure

title: Delhi Metro Phase IV receives government approval worth Rs 24,948 crore
text: New Delhi (PTI) - The Union Cabinet approved the Phase IV expansion of the Delhi Metro Rail Corporation network at an estimated cost of Rs 24,948 crore. The approved portion covers three corridors spanning a total length of 65.1 kilometres and 45 stations. The expansion aims to provide connectivity to several unserved and underserved areas of the national capital including Janakpuri West-RK Ashram Marg and Aerocity-Tughlakabad-Saket.
subject: Infrastructure

title: India opens Atal Tunnel, world's longest highway tunnel above 10000 feet
text: Manali (PTI) - Prime Minister Narendra Modi inaugurated the Atal Tunnel, the world's longest highway tunnel above 10,000 feet altitude, connecting the Manali valley to the Lahaul-Spiti valley in Himachal Pradesh. The 9.02-kilometre tunnel provides all-weather connectivity to the Lahaul-Spiti valley, which was previously cut off for about six months every year due to heavy snowfall. The tunnel was built by the Border Roads Organisation at a cost of Rs 3,200 crore.
subject: Infrastructure

title: India suspends Indus Waters Treaty negotiations with Pakistan after Pahalgam attack
text: New Delhi (PTI) - India announced the suspension of the Indus Waters Treaty with Pakistan and the closure of the Attari-Wagah land border crossing following the terrorist attack in Pahalgam that killed 26 tourists. The government also recalled its High Commissioner from Islamabad and ordered Pakistani diplomats in India to leave. External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar briefed the ambassadors of P5 countries and other major nations about India's decision.
subject: International

title: India conducts Operation Ganga to evacuate citizens from Ukraine
text: New Delhi (PTI) - India launched Operation Ganga to evacuate Indian nationals, primarily students, stranded in Ukraine following Russia's military invasion. Special flights were arranged and Indian Air Force aircraft were deployed to bring back citizens from neighbouring countries where they had taken refuge. Over 20,000 Indians were evacuated. Prime Minister Modi held multiple calls with the presidents of Russia and Ukraine urging the safety of Indian nationals.
subject: International

title: India and US sign BECA defence agreement during 2+2 dialogue
text: New Delhi (PTI) - India and the United States signed the Basic Exchange and Cooperation Agreement for Geospatial Intelligence during the third India-US 2+2 Ministerial Dialogue, completing the set of four foundational defence agreements between the two countries. The agreement enables sharing of advanced geospatial intelligence that will enhance the accuracy of automated systems and weapons. The signing was attended by the foreign and defence ministers of both countries.
subject: International

title: India wins hosting rights for 2036 Summer Olympics
text: New Delhi (PTI) - The International Olympic Committee awarded India the right to host the 2036 Summer Olympic Games, a landmark achievement for the country which would be hosting the Games for the first time. The announcement was made by IOC President Thomas Bach. Prime Minister Narendra Modi had personally lobbied for India's candidature. Ahmedabad and other Indian cities will host the events. The Games are expected to generate massive infrastructure investment.
subject: International

title: India signs free trade agreement with UAE boosting bilateral trade
text: New Delhi (PTI) - India and the United Arab Emirates signed a Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement, the first such trade deal signed by India in over a decade. The CEPA will provide duty-free access for 90 percent of Indian products to the UAE market. India aims to double bilateral trade to 100 billion dollars within five years. Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal said the agreement would create over a million jobs in India.
subject: Business

title: SEBI cracks down on front-running cases in mutual fund industry
text: Mumbai (PTI) - The Securities and Exchange Board of India took action against fund managers and dealers in multiple asset management companies for front-running, a practice where traders use advance knowledge of pending orders to profit personally. SEBI barred several individuals from the securities market and imposed financial penalties. The regulator said it was using surveillance systems and big data analytics to detect such market manipulation.
subject: Business

title: India opens Kartarpur Corridor allowing pilgrims visa-free access to Pakistan shrine
text: Kartarpur (PTI) - Prime Minister Narendra Modi inaugurated the Kartarpur Corridor allowing Indian Sikh pilgrims to visit the Gurdwara Darbar Sahib in Pakistan's Kartarpur without a visa. The corridor was opened days ahead of the 550th birth anniversary of Guru Nanak Dev. Pakistani Prime Minister Imran Khan attended the inauguration on the Pakistan side. The corridor had been a long-standing demand of the Sikh community.
subject: International

title: RBI launches digital rupee pilot programme
text: Mumbai (PTI) - The Reserve Bank of India launched a pilot of the Central Bank Digital Currency in the wholesale segment, marking India's entry into the world of sovereign digital currency. The e-rupee is a digital token that represents legal tender. In the retail segment pilot, select banks in select cities participated in a closed user group. The RBI said the CBDC would supplement and not replace physical cash.
subject: Business

title: India bans export of non-basmati white rice to stabilise domestic prices
text: New Delhi (PTI) - The Indian government imposed a ban on exports of non-basmati white rice with immediate effect to boost domestic availability and keep prices under control. The ban came as domestic rice prices rose sharply and the country faced the risk of food inflation. India is the world's largest rice exporter accounting for about 40 percent of global rice trade. The ban affected several Asian and African countries that depend on Indian rice imports.
subject: Economy

title: Supreme Court stays bulldozer demolitions, issues guidelines to states
text: New Delhi (PTI) - The Supreme Court of India stayed demolition drives being conducted by state governments against properties allegedly belonging to accused persons without following due process of law, calling the practice unconstitutional. The court said no authority could demolish a person's house merely because they were accused of a crime. It issued detailed guidelines requiring advance notice and opportunity to be heard before any demolition.
subject: Judiciary

title: India hosts SCO summit under its rotating presidency
text: New Delhi (PTI) - India hosted the Shanghai Cooperation Organisation summit under its chairmanship in virtual format, with leaders of member countries participating remotely. Prime Minister Narendra Modi chaired the meeting. The summit adopted the New Delhi Declaration. China's President Xi Jinping and Pakistan's Prime Minister participated virtually. The meeting focused on counterterrorism, economic connectivity, and regional security.
subject: International

title: Harnaaz Sandhu wins Miss Universe 2021 crown for India
text: Eilat (PTI) - Harnaaz Sandhu from Punjab won the Miss Universe 2021 title, becoming only the third Indian woman to be crowned Miss Universe after Sushmita Sen in 1994 and Lara Dutta in 2000. Sandhu, a model and actress, represented India at the 70th Miss Universe pageant held in Eilat, Israel. She defeated contestants from 79 other countries. Her answer on combating peer pressure and social media was particularly appreciated.
subject: Social

title: India launches PM SVANidhi scheme to support street vendors post-COVID
text: New Delhi (PTI) - The government launched the PM Street Vendor's AtmaNirbhar Nidhi scheme to provide affordable working capital loans to street vendors whose livelihoods were adversely affected by the COVID-19 lockdowns. Under the scheme, vendors can avail loans of up to Rs 10,000 initially with the option to graduate to higher credit limits upon timely repayment. A digital platform was created for vendor registration and loan disbursement.
subject: Social

title: India scores hat-trick of Oscars with wins at Academy Awards
text: Los Angeles (PTI) - India achieved a historic moment at the Academy Awards when multiple Indian productions and professionals received Oscars in the same year. The documentary Elephant Whisperers won in the documentary short category while Naatu Naatu from the Telugu film RRR won in the original song category. MM Keeravani composed and Chandrabose wrote the lyrics. It was one of India's most successful nights at the Oscars.
subject: Social

title: Narendra Modi sworn in as Prime Minister for third consecutive term (Report 1)
text: New Delhi (PTI) - Narendra Modi was sworn in as Prime Minister of India for the third consecutive time on June 9, 2024, at Rashtrapati Bhavan. President Droupadi Murmu administered the oath of office to Modi, who led the National Democratic Alliance to victory in the 2024 general elections. The NDA secured 293 seats in the 543-member Lok Sabha. Several Cabinet ministers were also sworn in during the ceremony. The swearing-in was attended by leaders from neighbouring countries including Bangladesh, Sri Lanka, Nepal, and the Maldives as part of India's neighbourhood-first foreign policy.
subject: Politics

title: Parliament passes Women's Reservation Bill guaranteeing 33 percent seats (Report 2)
text: New Delhi (PTI) - The Indian Parliament passed the Constitution (One Hundred and Twenty-Eighth Amendment) Bill, 2023, commonly known as the Women's Reservation Bill, which reserves 33 percent of seats in the Lok Sabha and state legislative assemblies for women. The Rajya Sabha passed the bill with 214 votes in favour and no opposition. Prime Minister Narendra Modi called it a historic moment for Indian democracy. The law will come into effect after the next delimitation exercise.
subject: Politics

title: Congress wins Karnataka assembly elections securing 135 seats (Report 3)
text: Bengaluru (PTI) - The Indian National Congress won a decisive majority in the Karnataka assembly elections, securing 135 seats in the 224-member assembly, according to the Election Commission of India. The Bharatiya Janata Party was reduced to 66 seats while the Janata Dal (Secular) won 19 seats. Congress leader Siddaramaiah was later sworn in as Chief Minister. The results marked a significant setback for the BJP which had governed the state.
subject: Politics

title: Arvind Kejriwal arrested by Enforcement Directorate in Delhi liquor policy case (Report 4)
text: New Delhi (PTI) - Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal was arrested by the Enforcement Directorate on Thursday in connection with the alleged Delhi excise policy scam. This made Kejriwal the first sitting Chief Minister to be arrested in India. The ED alleged that proceeds of crime were used to fund election campaigns. The Aam Aadmi Party called the arrest politically motivated. The Delhi High Court later examined his custody conditions.
subject: Politics

title: BJP wins Uttar Pradesh assembly elections with massive majority (Report 5)
text: Lucknow (PTI) - The Bharatiya Janata Party won a massive majority in the Uttar Pradesh assembly elections, securing 255 seats out of 403. The Samajwadi Party won 111 seats. Yogi Adityanath was sworn in as Chief Minister for the second term. The election result was seen as a significant endorsement of the BJP's governance in the state and a strong indicator ahead of the 2024 general elections.
subject: Politics

title: India elects Droupadi Murmu as 15th President, first tribal woman to hold office (Report 6)
text: New Delhi (PTI) - Droupadi Murmu was elected as the 15th President of India, becoming the first tribal woman and the second woman to hold the country's highest constitutional office. She defeated opposition candidate Yashwant Sinha by securing 64 percent of the total valid votes. Born in a tribal family in Odisha's Mayurbhanj district, Murmu was the former Governor of Jharkhand. She was sworn in on July 25, 2022.
subject: Politics

title: Rahul Gandhi disqualified from Lok Sabha after conviction in defamation case (Report 7)
text: New Delhi (PTI) - Congress leader Rahul Gandhi was disqualified from the Lok Sabha after a Surat court convicted him in a 2019 criminal defamation case over his Modi surname remarks. The disqualification came into effect immediately after the conviction. Gandhi later appealed against the conviction in the Supreme Court, which stayed the conviction and restored his membership. The episode triggered a political controversy across the country.
subject: Politics

title: Election Commission announces 2024 Lok Sabha election schedule across seven phases (Report 8)
text: New Delhi (PTI) - The Election Commission of India announced the schedule for the 18th General Elections to be held in seven phases between April 19 and June 1, 2024. The counting of votes was scheduled for June 4, 2024. The announcement set in motion the Model Code of Conduct across the country. Over 960 million voters were eligible to participate in the elections, making it the world's largest democratic exercise.
subject: Politics

title: AAP sweeps Delhi assembly elections winning 62 of 70 seats (Report 9)
text: New Delhi (PTI) - The Aam Aadmi Party led by Arvind Kejriwal swept the Delhi assembly elections, winning 62 of the 70 seats. The BJP won eight seats while the Congress drew a blank for the second consecutive time. Kejriwal was sworn in as Chief Minister for the third time. The result was seen as a strong mandate for AAP's governance model focused on free electricity, water, and improvements in government schools and hospitals.
subject: Politics

title: TMC wins West Bengal assembly elections, Mamata Banerjee retains power (Report 10)
text: Kolkata (PTI) - The Trinamool Congress won the West Bengal assembly elections for the third consecutive time, securing 213 seats in the 294-seat assembly. The BJP, which had mounted an aggressive campaign, won 77 seats. Mamata Banerjee was sworn in as Chief Minister for the third term. The election was marked by violence in several districts and high voter turnout. The Supreme Court took note of post-poll violence in the state.
subject: Politics

title: Modi government launches PM Kisan Samman Nidhi scheme for farmers (Report 11)
text: New Delhi (PTI) - Prime Minister Narendra Modi launched the Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi scheme, under which eligible farmer families would receive Rs 6,000 per year in direct income support, paid in three equal instalments. The scheme was announced in the interim budget and was made applicable to all farmer families across the country. The government said the scheme would benefit approximately 120 million farming families across India.
subject: Politics

title: India and China agree to disengage troops from all friction points in eastern Ladakh (Report 12)
text: New Delhi (PTI) - India and China reached an agreement on patrolling arrangements along the Line of Actual Control in eastern Ladakh, effectively ending the over four-year military standoff that began in May 2020. The Indian Army confirmed that disengagement had been completed at the Depsang and Demchok friction points. The agreement was announced by the Ministry of External Affairs. The two countries agreed to resume normal patrolling activities.
subject: Politics

title: Amit Shah introduces Citizenship Amendment Act in Parliament (Report 13)
text: New Delhi (PTI) - Home Minister Amit Shah introduced the Citizenship Amendment Bill in the Lok Sabha, which proposed to grant Indian citizenship to persecuted minorities from Pakistan, Bangladesh and Afghanistan. The bill was passed by the Lok Sabha with 311 votes in favour and 80 against. The bill excluded Muslims from its ambit, triggering widespread protests across the country particularly in the Northeast and universities.
subject: Politics

title: Supreme Court upholds abrogation of Article 370 in Jammu and Kashmir (Report 14)
text: New Delhi (PTI) - The Supreme Court of India unanimously upheld the constitutional validity of the abrogation of Article 370, which had granted special status to Jammu and Kashmir. A five-judge constitution bench led by Chief Justice D Y Chandrachud also directed the Election Commission to hold assembly elections in J&K by September 30, 2024. The court said the President had the power to abrogate Article 370.
subject: Politics

title: India's Opposition forms INDIA alliance ahead of 2024 general elections (Report 15)
text: Mumbai (PTI) - Twenty-six opposition parties came together to form the Indian National Developmental Inclusive Alliance, abbreviated as INDIA, to contest the 2024 general elections jointly against the ruling BJP-led NDA. The alliance was announced after a meeting in Mumbai. The member parties included the Indian National Congress, Trinamool Congress, DMK, Aam Aadmi Party, Samajwadi Party, NCP, Shiv Sena and others.
subject: Politics

title: Pulwama terror attack kills 40 CRPF jawans in Jammu and Kashmir (Report 16)
text: Pulwama (PTI) - A suicide bomber rammed an explosive-laden vehicle into a CRPF convoy on the Jammu-Srinagar National Highway at Pulwama in Jammu and Kashmir, killing at least 40 paramilitary personnel and injuring several others. Pakistan-based terrorist organisation Jaish-e-Mohammed claimed responsibility for the attack. The attack triggered massive outrage across the country and led to severe strain in India-Pakistan relations. The government vowed strong action against the perpetrators.
subject: Security

title: Indian Air Force conducts airstrikes on Balakot terror camp in Pakistan (Report 17)
text: New Delhi (PTI) - The Indian Air Force conducted airstrikes on a Jaish-e-Mohammed terrorist training camp in Balakot in Pakistan's Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province, in retaliation for the Pulwama terror attack. The strikes were carried out in the early hours and were described as a non-military preemptive action. Foreign Secretary Vijay Gokhale briefed ambassadors in New Delhi about the strikes. Pakistan denied any damage to the camp.
subject: Security

title: Uri army base attack kills 18 soldiers, India launches surgical strikes (Report 18)
text: Uri (PTI) - Militants attacked an Indian Army brigade headquarters at Uri in Jammu and Kashmir's Baramulla district, killing 18 soldiers and injuring 19 others in one of the deadliest attacks on the armed forces in recent years. The militants stormed the base and set fire to tents before being neutralised. Following the attack, Indian Army special forces conducted surgical strikes across the Line of Control against launch pads used by militants.
subject: Security

title: NIA arrests key conspirator in 2006 Mumbai train blasts case (Report 19)
text: Mumbai (PTI) - The National Investigation Agency arrested a key conspirator in the July 11, 2006 Mumbai suburban railway serial bomb blasts that killed 188 people and injured more than 800 others. The seven coordinated explosions occurred within 11 minutes across the Western Railway line during the evening rush hour. Several accused including members of the Student Islamic Movement of India and Lashkar-e-Taiba were convicted by a special court.
subject: Security

title: Pathankot airbase attack foiled, five terrorists killed by security forces (Report 20)
text: Pathankot (PTI) - Security forces killed five terrorists who had attacked the Pathankot Air Force Station in Punjab in a sustained operation lasting several days. The terrorists had entered India from Pakistan and targeted the strategic airbase. Two security personnel were also killed in the operation. The National Investigation Agency probed the attack and the government summoned the Pakistani High Commissioner to lodge a strong protest.
subject: Security

title: Maoist attack kills 22 CRPF personnel in Chhattisgarh's Sukma district (Report 21)
text: Raipur (PTI) - A group of Maoist insurgents ambushed and killed 22 CRPF personnel in Sukma district of Chhattisgarh in one of the deadliest attacks on security forces in the region. The personnel were on a road opening patrol when they were ambushed by the CPI Maoist guerrillas. The attack triggered a massive combing operation in the region. Senior officials said the security forces had been lured into the ambush.
subject: Security

title: Terrorist attack on Pahalgam kills 26 tourists in Jammu and Kashmir (Report 22)
text: Pahalgam (PTI) - Terrorists opened fire on tourists at Baisaran meadow near Pahalgam in Anantnag district of Jammu and Kashmir, killing 26 people including tourists from various states and a local guide. The attack was one of the deadliest on civilians in the valley in two decades. Security forces launched a massive manhunt for the attackers. The incident triggered nationwide outrage and a diplomatic crisis with Pakistan.
subject: Security

title: Coimbatore car bomb blast kills one and injures several near temple (Report 23)
text: Coimbatore (PTI) - A car exploded near the Kottai Eachanari Vinayagar Temple in Coimbatore killing the lone occupant of the vehicle and injuring several bystanders. The National Investigation Agency took over the probe and arrested multiple persons with alleged links to the Popular Front of India. The case was linked to an alleged larger conspiracy. The police said the incident was a case of a vehicle being used as an improvised explosive device.
subject: Security

title: Security forces kill top Hizbul Mujahideen commander in Kashmir encounter (Report 24)
text: Srinagar (PTI) - Security forces killed a top commander of the Hizbul Mujahideen militant organisation in an encounter in the Sopore area of north Kashmir. The militant commander was among those responsible for several attacks on security forces and civilians in the valley. The Army and J&K Police jointly conducted the operation. The encounter lasted several hours before the security forces neutralised the militants.
subject: Security

title: India bans Popular Front of India under UAPA for alleged terror links (Report 25)
text: New Delhi (PTI) - The Indian government banned the Popular Front of India and its associated organisations for five years under the Unlawful Activities Prevention Act, citing alleged links with terrorist organisations and activities aimed at destabilising the country. The ban followed a nationwide crackdown in which hundreds of PFI members were arrested across multiple states. Home Minister Amit Shah said the organisation was involved in radicalisation of youth.
subject: Security

title: Manipur violence displaces thousands as ethnic clashes continue for months (Report 26)
text: Imphal (PTI) - Ethnic violence between the Meitei and Kuki-Zo communities in Manipur continued to displace thousands of people across the state as the conflict entered its second month. More than 200 people were killed and over 50,000 displaced in one of the worst episodes of internal violence in northeast India in decades. The army and central paramilitary forces were deployed in large numbers. The Supreme Court took suo motu cognisance of the situation.
subject: Security

title: India becomes world's fifth largest economy surpassing UK (Report 27)
text: New Delhi (PTI) - India overtook the United Kingdom to become the world's fifth largest economy in terms of GDP at current market prices, according to data from the International Monetary Fund. India's GDP stood at approximately 3.53 trillion dollars against the UK's 3.38 trillion dollars. The milestone was widely reported and celebrated as a sign of India's growing economic clout. India is now behind only the United States, China, Germany, and Japan.
subject: Economy

title: RBI raises repo rate by 50 basis points to tame inflation (Report 28)
text: Mumbai (PTI) - The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee raised the policy repo rate by 50 basis points to 5.9 percent in its ongoing effort to tame retail inflation which had remained above the 6 percent tolerance band for several months. RBI Governor Shaktikanta Das said the MPC voted unanimously to remain focused on the withdrawal of accommodation to ensure that inflation progressively aligns with the target. GDP growth forecast was revised.
subject: Economy

title: Government launches Rs 20 lakh crore economic package to fight COVID-19 impact (Report 29)
text: New Delhi (PTI) - Prime Minister Narendra Modi announced a special economic package of Rs 20 lakh crore, equivalent to about 10 percent of India's GDP, to help the country revive from the economic damage caused by the COVID-19 pandemic and the lockdown. The package was announced by Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman in five tranches over several days. It included measures for MSMEs, migrant workers, farmers, and the financial sector.
subject: Economy

title: India's GDP contracts 23.9 percent in first quarter due to COVID lockdown (Report 30)
text: New Delhi (PTI) - India's gross domestic product contracted by 23.9 percent in the April-June quarter of 2020-21, the worst quarterly decline since the country began publishing quarterly GDP data, according to the National Statistical Office. The contraction was attributed to the nationwide lockdown imposed to contain the spread of COVID-19. The manufacturing sector contracted by 39.3 percent while the construction sector contracted by 50.3 percent.
subject: Economy

title: Adani Group stocks lose Rs 10 lakh crore market value after Hindenburg report (Report 31)
text: Mumbai (PTI) - Shares of Adani Group companies lost a combined market capitalisation of over Rs 10 lakh crore in a matter of days following the release of a report by US short-seller Hindenburg Research alleging stock manipulation and accounting fraud. The Adani Group denied all allegations and called the report a malicious combination of selective misinformation. The Supreme Court later constituted an expert committee to examine the matter.
subject: Economy

title: Government introduces new labour codes consolidating 44 central laws (Report 32)
text: New Delhi (PTI) - The Indian Parliament passed four new labour codes that consolidate 44 existing central labour laws into a simplified framework. The codes cover wages, industrial relations, social security, and occupational safety. Trade unions raised concerns about certain provisions including changes to conditions for layoffs and strikes in larger establishments. The government said the reforms would improve ease of doing business and protect worker rights.
subject: Economy

title: Sensex hits all-time high of 80,000 points for the first time in history (Report 33)
text: Mumbai (PTI) - The BSE Sensex crossed the historic milestone of 80,000 points for the first time, reflecting strong investor confidence in the Indian economy. The rally was led by gains in banking, information technology, and fast-moving consumer goods stocks. Analysts attributed the rise to strong corporate earnings, robust foreign institutional investor inflows, and expectations of political stability following the general elections. The Nifty 50 also hit a record high.
subject: Economy

title: India's forex reserves touch record high of 704 billion dollars (Report 34)
text: Mumbai (PTI) - India's foreign exchange reserves touched a record high of 704.88 billion dollars, according to data released by the Reserve Bank of India. The increase was attributed to a rise in foreign currency assets as well as gold reserves. Analysts said the comfortable level of reserves provided adequate import cover and acted as a buffer against external shocks. India ranks among the top five countries globally in terms of foreign exchange reserves.
subject: Economy

title: GST collections cross Rs 2 lakh crore mark for the first time (Report 35)
text: New Delhi (PTI) - Goods and Services Tax collections crossed the Rs 2 lakh crore mark for the first time in April 2024, according to the Finance Ministry. The collections stood at Rs 2.10 lakh crore, reflecting strong economic activity and improved compliance. Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman attributed the milestone to increased consumption and better tax administration. GST was introduced in July 2017 replacing multiple state and central indirect taxes.
subject: Economy

title: Nirmala Sitharaman presents sixth consecutive Union Budget (Report 36)
text: New Delhi (PTI) - Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman presented the Union Budget for 2024-25, her sixth consecutive budget and a record for any finance minister in India. The budget increased capital expenditure to Rs 11.11 lakh crore and announced significant tax relief for the middle class under the new tax regime. She announced increased allocations for infrastructure, agriculture, and education. The fiscal deficit was pegged at 5.1 percent of GDP.
subject: Economy

title: India reports over four lakh COVID cases in a single day at second wave peak (Report 37)
text: New Delhi (PTI) - India reported a record 4,14,188 new COVID-19 cases in a single day as the second wave of the pandemic swept across the country, overwhelming the healthcare system. Hospitals in major cities including Delhi and Mumbai faced acute shortages of oxygen, ICU beds, and critical medicines. The death toll also reached a record high with 3,915 deaths reported in a single day. Multiple states imposed lockdowns and curfews to control the spread.
subject: Health

title: India launches world's largest COVID-19 vaccination drive (Report 38)
text: New Delhi (PTI) - India launched the world's largest COVID-19 vaccination drive, beginning with the inoculation of healthcare workers across the country. Prime Minister Narendra Modi addressed the nation and declared the drive a success in the making. Two vaccines — Covishield developed by the Serum Institute of India and Covaxin developed by Bharat Biotech — were approved for emergency use. India aimed to vaccinate 300 million people by July 2021.
subject: Health

title: India imposes nationwide lockdown to contain COVID-19 spread (Report 39)
text: New Delhi (PTI) - Prime Minister Narendra Modi announced a complete nationwide lockdown for 21 days to contain the spread of COVID-19, giving citizens only four hours notice before the restrictions came into effect. The lockdown required all citizens to stay home except for essential services. It was one of the most stringent lockdowns in the world. The announcement triggered a mass exodus of migrant workers from cities to their home states.
subject: Health

title: India achieves milestone of 2 billion COVID-19 vaccine doses administered (Report 40)
text: New Delhi (PTI) - India achieved the milestone of administering 2 billion COVID-19 vaccine doses since the launch of its vaccination programme, making it the first country after China to cross this mark. The Union Health Ministry said this achievement was a testament to India's robust vaccination delivery infrastructure. Prime Minister Modi congratulated health workers and scientists. India had vaccinated the majority of its eligible adult population.
subject: Health

title: Omicron variant drives third wave of COVID across India (Report 41)
text: New Delhi (PTI) - India's third wave of COVID-19 driven by the Omicron variant saw daily case counts rise sharply across the country with major cities including Mumbai, Delhi, and Kolkata reporting the highest numbers. However, hospitalisation rates remained significantly lower than in the second wave. The government reimposed several restrictions including night curfews in multiple states. Health officials urged eligible citizens to receive booster doses.
subject: Health

title: Chandrayaan-3 successfully lands near moon's south pole, India makes history (Report 42)
text: Bengaluru (ISRO) - India's Chandrayaan-3 spacecraft successfully landed near the south pole of the Moon, making India the fourth country in the world and the first to land near the lunar south pole. The Vikram lander touched down and subsequently deployed the Pragyan rover, which began exploring the lunar surface. Prime Minister Modi, who was attending the BRICS summit in South Africa, joined the celebration virtually. ISRO scientists erupted in joy at the Mission Operations Complex.
subject: Science

title: ISRO successfully launches Aditya-L1 solar mission (Report 43)
text: Sriharikota (ISRO) - The Indian Space Research Organisation successfully launched the Aditya-L1 spacecraft, India's first space-based solar observatory, aboard the PSLV-C57 rocket from the Satish Dhawan Space Centre in Sriharikota. The spacecraft is destined for the Sun-Earth Lagrangian point 1, located approximately 1.5 million kilometres from Earth, from where it will study the Sun's outer atmosphere, solar winds, and solar flares.
subject: Science

title: Chandrayaan-2 orbiter continues to function successfully around moon (Report 44)
text: Bengaluru (ISRO) - India's Chandrayaan-2 orbiter continues to function successfully in lunar orbit nearly five years after it was launched, according to ISRO. While the Vikram lander crashed during its soft landing attempt in September 2019, the orbiter has been sending valuable data from its eight scientific instruments. ISRO said the orbiter has a mission life of several more years and has contributed significant data on the lunar surface and exosphere.
subject: Science

title: India successfully tests Agni-5 missile with multiple independently targetable warheads (Report 45)
text: New Delhi (PTI) - India successfully conducted a flight test of the Agni-5 ballistic missile equipped with Multiple Independently Targetable Re-entry Vehicle technology, becoming one of the few countries to possess this capability. MIRV technology allows a single missile to carry multiple warheads that can hit different targets. The test was conducted from APJ Abdul Kalam Island off the Odisha coast. Defence Minister Rajnath Singh congratulated DRDO and the armed forces.
subject: Science

title: IIT researchers develop low-cost COVID testing kit with 96 percent accuracy (Report 46)
text: New Delhi (PTI) - Researchers at the Indian Institute of Technology, Delhi developed a low-cost rapid diagnostic kit for detecting COVID-19 with an accuracy of 96 percent. The kit, which could deliver results within one hour at a fraction of the cost of standard RT-PCR tests, was submitted for regulatory approval to the Indian Council of Medical Research. The development was funded by the Department of Science and Technology and offered hope for mass testing in rural areas.
subject: Science

title: ISRO's PSLV-C55 successfully places TeLEOS-2 and Lumelite-4 in orbit (Report 47)
text: Sriharikota (ISRO) - India's Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle PSLV-C55 successfully placed Singapore's TeLEOS-2 and Lumelite-4 satellites into their designated orbits from the Satish Dhawan Space Centre.
